channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Lally AI Academy: Build & Deploy an AI Product in 30 Days
item.description: Expanding your portfolio? Curious about Claude Code? Join a 30-day sprint where your team designs, builds, and deploys a live product using AI and professional-grade tools. Open to all majors, no coding needed. Teams of 3–6 (at least 1 Lally student for commercial orientation, and a faculty/staff mentor) receive up to $500 in AI tools, digital services, and makerspace access. Earn a certificate, practice teamwork, time management, and resource management. Start on or after April 1.
item.author: Students

item.title: Still Accepting Applications for CSTEP!
item.description: The Collegiate Science & Technology Entry Program (CSTEP) is excited to announce that applications for the Spring semester are now open! CSTEP is an academic enrichment program designed to support New York State residents who are economically disadvantaged and pursuing careers in STEM or health-related fields.
item.author: Students

item.title: Cap & Gown Information
item.description: Cap & Gown Information can be found here: https://administration.rpi.edu/collegiate-store/commencement Deadlines: Bachelor or Master Degrees: If you wish to participate in the early May pickup event order online no later than Friday, March 27, 2026. If you want the regalia shipped to you, order online no later than Thursday April 9, 2026. Doctorate Degrees: Friday, March 20, 2026 For additional questions, reach out to: RPI@bkstr.com
item.author: Students

item.title: School of Architecture Co-terminal Information Session
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 10, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - Are you interested in opportunities to pursue graduate studies? Join us for a slice of pizza and a chance to hear about the different master’s degrees that the School of Architecture offers at one of the upcoming information sessions. We’ll talk about the benefits of co-terminal programs and the professional benefits of graduating with a master’s degree. Interested students are encouraged to attend. The co-terminal application deadline is March…
item.author: Students

item.title: School of Architecture Co-terminal Information Session
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 12:30 PM - 1:30 PM - Are you interested in opportunities to pursue graduate studies? Join us for a slice of pizza and a chance to hear about the different master’s degrees that the School of Architecture offers at one of the upcoming information sessions. We’ll talk about the benefits of co-terminal programs and the professional benefits of graduating with a master’s degree. Interested students are encouraged to attend. The co-terminal application deadline is…
item.author: Students

item.title: Etiquette Luncheon (RSVP required)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 1:30 PM - 4:00 PM - Gain confidence in a professional dining setting through this low pressure etiquette luncheon. Connect with campus and community partners during a networking session followed by a seated lunch and guided presentation from Center for Career and Professional Development, Access & Neurodiverse Student Success, and 1824 Catering. RSVP via Handshake.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tutoring for Chemistry I, II, and Organic
item.description: Did you know the Success Center offers small group tutoring in Chemistry I, II and Organic Chemistry SEVEN days a week! Check out our small group tutoring schedule and sign up today with our amazing peer tutors: https://rpi.mywconline.com/.
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense
item.description: Date: Monday, March 9, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - PhD Candidate: Chris Jerrett Advisor: Prof. Elliot Anshelevich Date: March 9, 2026 When: 10:00 am Where: Sage 5101 or https://rensselaer.webex.com/meet/jerrec
item.author: Students

item.title: Concert on March 11 - CODE POEMS: Migrations and Angels
item.description: The electroacoustic duo CODE POEMS, featuring Matt Sargent (composition, guitar, electronics) and Taylor Long (percussion), is dedicated to performing experimental works by living composers. The program features two pieces written expressly for the ensemble: Matthew Goodheart’s “Migrations,” for percussion and lap steel with multichannel electronics, and Matt Sargent’s “Several Angels,” for percussion and electric guitar with real-time scoring. Join at RPI Chapel and Cultural Center from 7-8:30.
item.author: Community

item.title: Mare Donohue’s Retirement Celebration
item.description: Date: Friday, March 27th, 2026 12:30-2:30 PM - After nine years of dedicated service to RPI, Mare Donohue, Manager of Client Services Support, will retire at the end of March. As the face of IT, Mare has led the Help Desk with care and commitment, positively impacting countless students, faculty, and staff. Please join us in celebrating her career on Friday, March 27, from 12:30–2:30 PM at the Heffner Alumni House. Kindly RSVP: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/W9WxAqbk2j
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty Spotlight on Research & Impact webinar series-Jennifer Pazour
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 2:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Systems that Amplify What if we could better harness people’s willingness to contribute? While humans are often eager to help, without scaled coordination, their efforts don’t always translate into stronger outcomes. Prof. Pazour will talk about how Industrial & Systems Engineering can amplify human contributions through the design of new systems, optimization approaches, and technologies that unlock underutilized capacity.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rπ Giving Day
item.description: Date: Saturday, March 14, 2026 (all day) - Join us in support of RPI students, teams and student-athletes, clubs and activities, fraternities and sororities, scholarships, schools, and our area of greatest need – the Rensselaer Annual Fund. The day will also feature a fun Rπ Giving Day πK run. Visit the website to learn how you can get involved!
item.author: Community

item.title: CEE Seminar Series - Dr. Weilan Zhang Assistant Professor University of Albany, State University of NY
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - Numerous sites across the country are currently receiving, or have previously received, PFAS-containing biosolids for land application. Such practice could lead to PFAS contamination in receiving soils and adjacent waters, threatening ecosystems and human health. Recently, we investigated the levels of PFAS in biosolid-amended soil, their migration to surrounding surface water bodies, and bioaccumulation in plants near land application sites…
item.author: Community

item.title: MSE & BME Joint Seminar: Dr. Abraham Joy
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - Development of a Peptide-like Biomaterial Platform: Bridging the Gap Between Natural and Synthetic Biomaterials Abraham Joy Northeastern University
item.author: Community

item.title: McKinney Writing Contest Open Through March 9
item.description: The 2026 McKinney Writing Contest is open from November 1, 2025 through March 9, 2026. Submit in any or all of the following categories: Fiction, Creative Nonfiction, or Drama; Poetry; Academic Essay; Language & Image; or our NEW category: Writing for Games. Undergraduate and graduate divisions. First prize $300; 2nd prize $175; and 3rd prize $75. Submit: https://hass.rpi.edu/mckinney. Current students only. Questions? McKinney@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Get Ready for your Exams in the Success Center
item.description: Join us March 9-12 to prepare for upcoming exams this week! Drop-in Sessions: Calculus II - 7-9pm Essence of Engineering Mechanics - 7-9pm Multivariable Calculus - 7-9pm IEA - 7-9pm Data Structures - 8-10pm
item.author: Community

item.title: Zendesk ticketing system requiring email verification effective 03/09/2026
item.description: In an effort to prevent spam attacks, effective 03/09/2026 the Zendesk ticketing system is requiring a one time email verification for each instance for those who submit requests and are not signed into the system. This new workflow will require anonymous (not signed‑in) individuals who submit requests to verify their email address before an active ticket is created.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 13: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday March 13 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: MANE Spring Seminar Series with Dr. Yiyang Sun
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 10:30 AM - 11:30 AM - “Harnessing the Hidden Patterns of Fluid Flows: Physics-Driven Control for Unsteady Aerodynamic Flows”, Dr. Yiyang Sun, Blue and Gold Distinguished Professor, Associate Dean of Grad and Post-Grad Education, Department of Biomedical Engineering, University of Delaware
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Mercer Lecture: “Ferroelectric Devices and Circuits for AI Hardware Design”
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Distinguished Mercer Lecture: “Ferroelectric Devices and Circuits for AI Hardware Design” with Prof. Shimeng Yu of Georgia Institute of Technology.Abstract and speaker bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: A Non-Variational Quantum Approach to Solving Combinatorial Optimization Problems: Iterative-QAOA
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Quantum computers introduce new opportunities for modeling and solving decision problems. In this talk, we will first discuss the unique positioning of Industrial Engineering in the quantum research landscape that motivates new directions at the intersection of application and theory. Then as an example, we will present a new algorithm, Iterative-QAOA, for combinational optimization problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy Colloquium: Dr. Julian Georg, RPI
item.description: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: The Cosmic Microwave Background Explained for Everyone Abstract: The Universe is permeated by photons that are cosmological in origin and almost as old as the Universe itself. They are what we call the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB). In this talk, I will describe how we observe the CMB and what information we can extract from it, as well as explain the physics behind it. This talk is explicitly aimed undergraduate students regardless of…
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “The Art of Clear and Confident Communication”
item.description: Join us for Dr. Mike Bechtle’s The Art of Clear and Confident Communication Leadercamp and leave empowered with immediately actional tools for communicating effectively in any situation. Two times: 9:00AM - 10:00AM and 12:00PM - 1:00PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “How to Stay Motivated When Work Gets Tough” March 10, 2026 9:00 AM- 10:00 AM & 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM
item.description:
Motivation often breaks down under pressure, when work becomes difficult, demanding, or uncertain. During this Leadercamp, participants will learn how to clarify what matters most, reconnect effort to identity and purpose, and design environments that support sustained performance. Through examples, reflection, and applied tools, attendees will understand how to navigate challenging work phases, maintain focus when progress feels slow, and continue moving forward without burnout.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
